challenge.title: Don't Talk Hard
challenge.desc: Find the entrance of a speakeasy in downtown Seattle with the name of an alcohol in it and with the name of something in which you sit to wash and lather your body. The entrance shouldn’t be easy to find though.
challenge.privateNotes: Bathtub Gin in Seattle

challenge.title: Climbing For Some Coffee
challenge.desc: Find a coffee store in the Pike Place Market area which you can only reach by climbing up at least one flight of stairs (or an elevator).
challenge.privateNotes: Storyville Coffee Pike Place

challenge.title: Pags
challenge.desc: Visit three different Pagliacci Pizza locations, all three of which must be in different cities.

challenge.title: Anger Translator Park
challenge.desc: Find a park that’s (kind of) named after Obama’s anger translator and visit it. (It might have an extraneous name tacked onto it as well - just ignore that.)
challenge.privateNotes: Luther Burbank Park in Mercer Island

challenge.title: H-Mart
challenge.desc: Visit three different H-Mart locations. At most two can be in Seattle. The M2M location in Capitol Hill does not count.
challenge.privateNotes:
Best ones:

- U-Dist
- Bellevue
- Redmond
- Downtown Seattle

challenge.title: Artsy Town
challenge.desc: Visit a place where all homeowners are members of the Western Academy of Beaux Arts. You must provide us with evidence that you are within the town limits.
challenge.privateNotes: Beaux Arts Village

challenge.title: Soaring High in the Apartments
challenge.desc: A Boeing 747-400 fuselage has been installed between two buildings somewhere in Seattle. Find it and take a selfie with it.
challenge.privateNotes:
1200 Stewart St

https://google.com/maps/place/Boeing+747+fuselage/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x549015005a9ff999:0x7532f9c590fff109?sa=X&ved=1t:242&ictx=111

challenge.title: Black Museums Matter
challenge.desc: Visit the exterior of a museum dedicated to the history of African Americans.
challenge.privateNotes: Northwest African American Museum

challenge.title: Teriyaki Town
challenge.desc: Seattle is known as the home of western-style teriyaki chicken. It should therefore come as no surprise that there is no shortage of teriyaki chicken restaurants in the Seattle area. Find five different teriyaki chicken restaurants, each of which must be at least 0.25 miles away from each other, and at least one of which must be on the east side of Lake Washington. To be considered a teriyaki chicken restaurant, they must have the word "Teriyaki" in their name.
challenge.privateNotes: To be considered a teriyaki chicken restaurant, they must have the word "Teriyaki" in their name.

challenge.title: Simon Says
challenge.desc: Simon Property Group is a prolific operator of malls across the United States. Visit a property which they own; it must be within 1 mile of a Link light rail station.
challenge.privateNotes: Northgate Mall

challenge.title: Speak Your Mind, Round 2
challenge.desc:
Red Square at the University of Washington is known for being a bastion of the First Amendment. Anyone can go to protest whenever they want, and it’s a common sight to behold for anyone there. Protest for 5 minutes at UW’s Red Square on a stupid topic while filming it.

Example: everyone should wear polka dot pajamas before going to sleep! (Do not use that example or you won’t earn any points.)

Depending on how stupid the topic is, the organizers may award you up to 2 additional points. (This is completely subjective.)

Important: Must not be on a real political topic, else 50 points will be deducted (yep, you even lose points!)

challenge.title: eduroam
challenge.desc: Take a photo of a group member’s phone with “eduroam” as a listed SSID on the Wi-Fi page. You need not be connected to eduroam.

challenge.title: Generally Growing
challenge.desc: GGP Retail is a prolific operator of malls across the United States. Physically visit three properties which they own; they each must be within 2 miles of a Link light rail station. At least one property must be outside King County.
challenge.privateNotes: Must be Westlake Center and the Alderwood Mall and Shops at Bravern

challenge.title: Le Labo
challenge.desc: Omg I LOVE the Le Labo Seoul scent! Unfortunately it’s not currently available outside of… well… Seoul. But please go and find some Le Labo scents for me, any kind, as long as it’s a scent! (Don’t actually buy it, just find it for me please.)

challenge.title: Overstate
challenge.desc:
Stand on top of grass which is located directly above an interstate highway. In other words, if you dug at this grass straight downwards, you would eventually fall onto the interstate. (Do not, under any circumstances, attempt to dig anything or enter the interstate by foot!) Take a selfie at this place with all team members present.

Remember: state highways are not interstate highways! SR 520 is a state highway.
challenge.privateNotes: Freeway Park. Note that the grassy lids above SR 520 such as Montlake and Evergreen Point do not count because SR 520 isn't an interstate highway.

challenge.title: Resy Credit
challenge.desc: I have some Resy credits which I need to spend before they expire in a few weeks. Please find me a restaurant which will count towards the Amex Resy credit (i.e. the $100 per quarter Resy credit on the Amex Platinum or the $50 per half-year Resy credit on the Amex Gold). Take a photo outside of it with your whole team and send it to us.
challenge.privateNotes: To check, go to https://useyourcredits.com/maps/resy/ and click "All Restaurants". If it's in that list, it counts.

challenge.title: Thrifty Transit Trekker
challenge.desc: Find an item of clothing which can cover at least 20% of your body and costs under $5 at a thrift store, and take a photo with it. The price tag must also be visible, but it can be sent in a separate photo.

challenge.title: It's ABoat Time
challenge.desc: Find any sort of boat floating in the water and take a selfie with it.

challenge.title: It's ABoot Time
challenge.desc: Capture a selfie with one boot AND a Canadian flag in the frame. Regular shoes are not boots. Displaying images of boots or the Canadian flag in your photo do not count.

challenge.title: AVA
challenge.desc:
Find two AVA apartment complexes, one of which must be located outside of the city of Seattle. Take a selfie in front of both of them.

The apartment must be branded "AVA" and not "Avalon"
challenge.privateNotes: AVA Capitol Hill and AVA Esterra Park

challenge.title: Grown at Home
challenge.desc: Visit two Homegrown locations. (Permanently closed locations do not count. Absolutely do not try to visit the former location in Food Hall 6.)
challenge.privateNotes: Should be in Seattle and Mercer Island

challenge.title: Grocery Store PokéStop
challenge.desc:
No, not that kind of Poké. Find a supermarket with a poké bar, take a photo with it, and send it to us. (Do not send us any screenshots of Pokémon Go.)

The bar must consist of making your own bowl, buffet style. It must be self serve.
challenge.privateNotes: Should be a Metropolitan Market (Met Market). There is one within walking distance of Mercer Island Station

challenge.title: I Want BJ's
challenge.desc: Don’t you just love getting BJ’s? Please go outside of one and take a photo with it.
challenge.privateNotes: The only remotely accessible BJ's Brewhouse and Restaurant in this game is located in Redmond Town Center

challenge.title: Book Conduit
challenge.desc:
Check out a book from one branch of the Seattle Public Library or King County Library System and return it to another branch of the same library system. DO NOT RETURN IT TO THE WRONG LIBRARY SYSTEM!!! Take a picture of you checking out the book from one library and also when you drop it in the return drop box at the other library.

A 100 point penalty will be assessed if the book is not returned by the end of the game or if it is returned to the wrong library system.

challenge.title: Tutti Frutti
challenge.desc:
Bellevue is home to several blueberry farms which are open for U-Pick. Although it might not be quite blueberry season yet (or maybe it is), go within 1,000 feet of a blueberry farm and take a picture of the farm.

As us city folk often cannot tell apart a farm from other less notable swaths of vegetation, you must also send us the location where your photo was taken as proof that you were within 1,000 feet of the correct place. (You cannot complete this challenge while riding a train or bus.)
challenge.privateNotes: Should be near South Bellevue Station

challenge.title: Scorching Rodent Season
challenge.desc: Find a mosaic which honors a rodent in a Seattle park.
challenge.privateNotes: Hot Rat Summer in Cal Anderson Park

challenge.title: Movie Triad
challenge.desc: Visit one AMC, one Regal, and one Cinemark movie theater.

challenge.title: Dave and Buster's
challenge.desc: Visit two Dave and Busterses. They cannot be on the same side of Lake Washington.
challenge.privateNotes: One is near Wilburton Station

challenge.title: Modern Family
challenge.desc: Take two pictures, one of a first- and one of a second-generation Link train car. (The first-gen train cars are manufactured by Kinki Sharyo, whereas the second-gen train cars are manufactured by Siemens.) You can either be inside or outside of the train. As long as your picture includes enough of the train car in view, Game Control will be able to tell what kind you are in; don’t worry.

challenge.title: Mon char est du Canada
challenge.desc: Find a car with a Canadian license plate and take a photo of it. (The photo’s metadata must clearly indicate the time the photo was taken and which camera was used. If you did not cheat, this won’t be a problem at all.)

challenge.title: H(eat)ed Rivalry
challenge.desc:
Go across the Seattle metropolitan area to find consumable goods which represent characters in Heated Rivalry, the hit Crave original now streaming in the U.S. on HBO Max.

In order for each of these tasks to count as complete:

  • At most two can be done north of the Ship Canal on the west side of Lake Washington
  • At most one can be done south of the Ship Canal on the west side of Lake Washington
  • At most two can be done on the east side of Lake Washington

challenge.title: a) Shane Hollander
challenge.desc:
Find a restaurant that has ginger ale in their written menu, go to the restaurant, and take a photo with the physical copy of the menu. If the restaurant refuses to give a physical menu, a photo with the digital one they provide will suffice, but the photo must be taken at the restaurant.

Be sure to tell us where you found the restaurant. Note the restrictions applied on locations from the Heated Rivalry challenge.
challenge.privateNotes: Flavored ginger ales also count

challenge.title: b) Ilya Rozanov
challenge.desc:
Find a restaurant or bar that has a brand of vodka, either produced in a former Soviet country or with a Russian name, in their written menu. Go to that restaurant and take a photo with the physical copy of the menu. If the restaurant refuses to give a physical menu, a photo with the digital one they provide will suffice. Svedka is not a valid vodka.

Be sure to tell us where you found the restaurant. Note the restrictions applied on locations from the Heated Rivalry challenge.
challenge.privateNotes: Vodkas like Stolichnaya, which are produced in Latvia, would count.

challenge.title: c) Rose Landry
challenge.desc:
Find a restaurant that serves a dish which either consists of, or contains, a crap ton of fries in a written menu.

Be sure to tell us where you found the restaurant. Note the restrictions applied on locations from the Heated Rivalry challenge.
challenge.privateNotes: The amount of fries provided must be at least the size of a McDonald's large fries.

challenge.title: d) Scott Hunter
challenge.desc:
Find a dining establishment that serves either freshly made-to-order blueberry, pineapple, or kale smoothies, or any combination thereof, as indicated on a written menu or display board physically located at said establishment.

Be sure to tell us where you found the establishment. Note the restrictions applied on locations from the Heated Rivalry challenge.

challenge.title: e) Shilya Hollanov
challenge.desc:
Find a dining establishment, grocery store, deli, or convenience store which sells tuna melts, either premade or by order. Take a photo of the physical food or the physical menu where this is clearly listed.

Be sure to tell us where you found the store. Note the restrictions applied on locations from the Heated Rivalry challenge.
challenge.privateNotes: Must be a tuna melt

challenge.title: Hotpot pt. 2
challenge.desc: Visit two hotpot restaurants of the same brand. They must each be on different sides of Lake Washington. Take a selfie in front of them and a photo/screenshot of their menu.

challenge.title: Hot Pot pt. 2
challenge.desc: Visit two cannabis dispensaries and take a selfie in front of the store. (You must be within 10 feet of the storefront.) They must each be on different sides of Lake Washington.

challenge.title: Lily and Jane
challenge.desc: Find a business establishment within the game area with either the name “Lily” or “Jane” in them, go to them, and take a selfie in front of them. The establishments must allow members of the public to enter.

challenge.title: Just Can't Get Enough
challenge.desc:
Take a photo with the logos of Amazon, Microsoft, and Boeing wherever they appear in the wild. All three must be found before this task can be counted as complete.

(Your employee badge does not count as the wild.)

challenge.title: Enjoy the Silence
challenge.desc: Capture a video of three consecutive seconds of near silence while on the Link light rail. If the train makes a particularly loud noise, or if there are any noises from passengers, you’ll need to start over. Do not try to muffle the microphone to mute the microphone.
challenge.privateNotes: General train moving noises are fine, but if there's a curve that causes a screech on the tracks, that would be a disqualifying noise.

challenge.title: People Are People
challenge.desc:
As Martin Gore once said, “people are people so why should it be that you and I should get along so awfully”. Discuss with another team, in person, and find something you both disagree about. It cannot be a political/religious/etc. issue. Both of your teams must send us what you disagree on and the team whom you disagree with.

Additionally, you must tell us what this challenge name is a reference to (hint: it’s related to Enjoy the Silence, Just Can’t Get Enough, and Too Many People). Note, you can help another team even if you’ve already completed this challenge.
challenge.privateNotes: This is a reference to a Depeche Mode song.

challenge.title: People Aren't People
challenge.desc: Take photos with three statues of people, each of which must be at least 0.25 mile apart from each other.

challenge.title: Everything Counts
challenge.desc:
In the wise words of Martin Gore, “everything counts in large amounts”. As he also once said, “the grabbing hands grab all they can”. Go into a store and grab at least four separate things with just one hand, and take a photo with it.

Try not to grab anything brittle or whose sale value may be diminished if touched. You need not buy anything. (Certainly please don’t steal anything.)

challenge.title: I Can't Use My Amex Hotel Credits!
challenge.desc:
Did you know the Amex Platinum credit card gives $300 of hotel credit every half year? The catch is, it must be used to book rooms in hotels belonging to either The Hotel Collection (THC) or Fine Hotels and Resorts (FHR). Find two FHR hotels (we will NOT accept THC) in Seattle and take selfies in front of them.

For those of us without Amex Platinum cards, here's a list of FHR hotels in the Seattle area:

  • Lotte Hotel
  • 1 Hotel
  • Fairmont Olympic Hotel
  • Four Seasons Hotel
  • Hotel 1000
  • Populus

challenge.title: Defective Signage
challenge.desc: Find a sign displaying incorrect information or a defective electronic info screen at any Sound Transit station or aboard any Sound Transit Link train or King County Metro bus. Take a photo with it.

challenge.title: Bilt rent day is STILL a SCAM. They advertise it as 6% back but in reality it's 3% back twice! Does this make a difference? YES. E.G. a $19.52 transaction gets 58 points because they round down from .585, on rent day rather than giving 6% which should be 117 points they give 3% (rounded down) twice. So you only get 116 points. This is fraud and I am outraged.
challenge.desc: Bilt Rent Day is now even more of a scam! Unfortunately, many apartment complexes are in on the scam. Find an apartment which has a “Bilt Alliance” sign displayed and take a photo with it.

challenge.title: Florida Man, Round 2
challenge.desc:
It may be the farthest state away from Washington State, but that doesn’t mean we can’t recreate the crazy here in Seattle.

Have one of your team members do one of the following actual Florida Man actions:

  • Married to three women at the same time: One member of your team should get down on one knee and propose to three other Transit Trek players, each on three different other teams not including yours, to marry them. (They need not be women; gender and orientation are irrelevant 🌈) You may speak with other teams for the purpose of completing this challenge.
  • Marry a Ferris wheel: One member of your team should find a Ferris wheel, go as close as possible to it, and try to marry it.

Record it as a video and send it to us. A man from Florida will decide whether your submission is acceptable or not.

challenge.title: County Crossing
challenge.desc: Record a video of your team crossing from one county into another while in motion on a train or bus.

challenge.title: I(Just)Ran So Far Away Because I Can’t Afford Gas Anymore
challenge.desc: Take a photo with a gas station sign which is selling regular unleaded gasoline for over $7.000 per gallon.

challenge.title: Keep the Dream Alive
challenge.desc:
We are fortunate enough to be able to play this game across the lake because of over half a century of activism, supported by millions of people, to bring light rail to the Eastside. Unfortunately, Seattleites are currently having trouble realizing that dream for the Ballard Link Extension.

Let’s do our part to show our support for this vital link (no pun intended) which will someday connect Ballardites directly with the central business district. Visit the approximate location of one of the proposed stations (per the Preferred Alternative listed on SoundTransit.org: https://www.soundtransit.org/system-expansion/ballard-link-extension) and take a selfie there.

Your selfie must be at a proposed new station. It cannot be along the existing Link tracks.
challenge.privateNotes: Dravus Street, Elliott Ave and the Magnolia Bridge, Denny Way, Seattle Center

challenge.title: Too Many People
challenge.desc: Go to a park in Seattle which doesn’t charge for admission. Take a group selfie with at least ten other non-Transit Trekkers in the frame. You cannot tell them to come take a photo with you.

challenge.title: The Hospital Rule
challenge.desc:
Go outside of any hospital and send us a picture of your team there. Then, while still at the hospital, request a calculus problem to solve. You can use Google and LLMs if you need to, but it will be faster to solve it yourself if you remember calculus. You must solve the problem correctly while still at the hospital to receive credit.

A hospital must have an Emergency Room.

challenge.title: This Restaurant's Name Did Not Age Well
challenge.desc:
When Gen Alpha changed the funny number, there was a fancy restaurant which unfortunately ended up sharing a name with the meme. Find this restaurant and film your team member performing the associated hand gesture in front of the restaurant.

You do not need to enter the restaurant; doing it from the nearest public place with the restaurant in view is acceptable.
challenge.privateNotes: Six Seven Restaurant at Pier 67.

challenge.title: Evergreen
challenge.desc: Find three separate places, excluding transit stations, containing "Evergreen" in the title (whether singular or plural). At most two of them can be in Seattle. You cannot use more than one location of the same chain store.

challenge.title: Evergreen Station
challenge.desc: Find a public transit station or stop with the word "Evergreen" in it. You need not stop or get off at it, but you must prove that you went through it by sending us a photo with your team at/near the station/stop.
challenge.privateNotes: Needs to be Evergreen Point Freeway Station. Buses like the 542, 545, and 255 work for this.

find.title: Hidden Gem
find.desc: Find the following extremely obscure little-known hidden gem in the greater Seattle area:

find.title: FAQ
find.desc: This picture has been on our FAQ page for two years. Find the exact place and angle this picture was taken from and recreate it with your team:

find.title: Soccer Tournament
find.desc: Some signs like the one below have popped up in the greater Seattle area in preparation for what Sound Transit calls a "soccer tournament." Find one and take a team selfie with it.

hard.title: In a Pickle
hard.desc:
Buy a pickle (it can be sliced in a jar) and send us a photo of it to indicate you’ve begun this challenge.

Then, bring it to a pickleball court. Take a photo with your pickle sitting on the pickleball court.

You will be reimbursed up to $5 for the pickle or pickle jar only if you start this challenge when allowed, not before.

You must bring the pickle jar to the finish line or you will receive a point penalty.
